user_message: Before my call with Coho Winery, build the analysis. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 16:59 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery whitespace analysis

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 10:21 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 13:51 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is whitespace analysis.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:10:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:13:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:17:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:29:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
